question.text: Vikram buys a stock at INR 200, receives a dividend of INR 8, and sells it at INR 230 after one year. What is the Holding Period Return (HPR)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** HPR = (Income + End Value - Begin Value) / Begin Value = (8 + 230 - 200) / 200 = 38/200 = 19.00%. The HPR captures both capital gain (15%) and dividend yield (4%). **The Trap:** 15.00% considers only the capital gain (30/200) and ignores the dividend. 18.00% adds the dividend to the selling price but divides by 210 (average of buy and sell). 16.52% divides the total gain by the ending value (38/230). **Cross-Exam Context:** HPR is foundational and tested across NISM Series V-A, VIII, X-A, and X-B exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Never ignore dividend income when calculating returns — for value stocks and REITs, dividend income can constitute over 50% of the total return.
